summary: Sprint 1 completed all data pipeline P0s (PitchingStats model, ingest scripts, Docker, pipeline CLI). The core blocker is now LLM integration -- generate_sql() and generate_answer() are still stubs. Without working LLM, the app cannot be tested, demoed, or used. Data coverage improved (pitching model added) but no data has been ingested yet for any season. The recommended Sprint 2 focus is: (1) wire LLM integration, (2) ingest 5 years of batting+pitching data, (3) build test suite to 80% coverage. These three items unlock everything else.

findings.title: LLM integration still stubbed -- core value proposition non-functional
findings.detail: generate_sql() raises NotImplementedError and generate_answer() returns str(rows). Sprint 1 focused on security/UX but did not advance the core feature. This must be Sprint 2's top priority. Anthropic SDK is already in requirements.txt.
findings.recommendation: Wire generate_sql() to Anthropic Claude (claude-sonnet model). Wire generate_answer() to produce human-readable summaries. This is the single highest-impact task.

findings.title: No data ingested -- pipeline exists but never executed
findings.detail: Pipeline scripts exist for batting and pitching but no data has been loaded into the database. The app needs at minimum 2020-2025 batting and pitching data to answer meaningful questions.
findings.recommendation: Run ingest_batting.py and ingest_pitching.py for seasons 2020-2025. This is an operational task, not a code change. Add --start-year/--end-year CLI support for batch ingestion.

findings.title: Test suite at 13% -- blocks confident deployment
findings.detail: Only 25 tests exist, all for SQL guard. No endpoint tests, no schema validation tests, no integration tests. Cannot confidently deploy LLM integration without tests proving the query pipeline works end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Build backend test suite targeting 80% coverage: conftest.py with async fixtures, test_main.py for endpoints, expanded test_text_to_sql.py for execute_sql and generate_answer.

findings.title: No Alembic migrations -- schema changes require manual intervention
findings.detail: create_all() in lifespan cannot handle column additions or type changes. As the schema evolves (new stat columns, new tables), migrations are essential. Alembic is installed but not initialized. Same as TD-013.
findings.recommendation: Initialize Alembic, create initial migration, replace create_all().

findings.title: Player profiles missing position and handedness from pipeline
findings.detail: Player model has position, bats, throws fields but ingest scripts only extract IDfg, Name, Team. Position data is useful for questions like 'Who are the best shortstops?' Same as PROD-008 from Sprint 1.
findings.recommendation: Update PLAYER_COLUMN_MAP in both ingest scripts to include position data from pybaseball output.

findings.title: No automated data refresh workflow
findings.detail: Data ingestion is manual-only (run script). During the MLB season, stats change daily. Without automated refresh, data becomes stale quickly.
findings.recommendation: Add a cron job or GitHub Action to run ingestion weekly during the season. Defer to P2 -- manual refresh is fine for MVP.

proposed_milestones.title: M1: Working Query Loop (Sprint 2 Focus)
proposed_milestones.rationale: Make the app actually functional. Users can ask questions and get real answers. This is the MVP milestone.
items: Wire generate_sql() to Anthropic Claude API
Wire generate_answer() to produce human-readable answers

proposed_milestones.title: M2: Data Depth + Polish (Sprint 3)
proposed_milestones.rationale: With LLM working, focus on data coverage and UX polish.

proposed_milestones.title: M3: Public Launch Readiness (Sprint 4)
proposed_milestones.rationale: Production hardening for sharing with real users.
